recalls.Description: Thrasio has announced a recall of Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Removers available in Fresh Clean Scent and Orange Twist Scent. These products were sold in 24-ounce, 32-ounce, and 1-gallon sizes, featuring orange and white bottles labeled with "Angry Orange" and "Stain & Odor Remover." Some items were also sold as part of a bundle with a UV light attachment for the spray bottle.
recalls.Title: Thrasio Issues Recall for Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Removers Due to Bacterial Contamination Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover immediately and contact Thrasio for a full refund. To initiate the refund process, take a photo of the product with the word "recalled" and your initials marked on it, then email the image to productrecall@angryorange.com. Dispose of the product in its original container with household trash without emptying it or recycling the bottle.

Products.Name: Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,500,000 (In addition, about 43,700 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Orange Twist Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover
Recalled Fresh Clean Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover
Recalled Orange Twist Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover Gallon
Recalled Orange Twist Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover & UV Light Bundle

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Target, The Home Depot, Meijer, Staples, TJ Maxx, and other major retailers, as well as online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com, AngryOrange.com, and Chewy.com from March 2019 through December 2025, priced between $4 and $60.

Hazards.Name: Risk of exposure to Pseudomonas aeruginosa bacteria

recalls.metaDescription: Thrasio recalls Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Removers due to bacterial contamination risk. Stop using and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: Creekwood Naturals is recalling its 100% Natural Pure Gum Spirits of Turpentine due to a serious safety risk. The product is packaged in clear glass bottles featuring a brown label with prominent red, black, and white lettering, clearly displaying the word 'Turpentine' along with 'Pure Gum Spirits' or 'Quality Gum Spirits.' This packaging does not comply with the Poison Prevention Packaging Act, creating a potential poisoning hazard for young children.
recalls.Title: Creekwood Naturals Recalls 100% Natural Pure Gum Spirits of Turpentine Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach Creekwood Naturals at 877-754-1776 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at Sales@CreekwoodNaturals.com. More information is available online at https://CreekwoodNaturals.com/pages/recall or by visiting https://CreekwoodNaturals.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: 100% Natural Pure Gum Spirits of Turpentine

Images.Caption: Recalled Creekwood Naturals 100% Natural Pure Gum Spirits Turpentine bottle

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon, Etsy, eBay, and Creekwood Naturals from June 2023 through May 2025, priced between $20 and $40.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the product away from children and request a free replacement child-resistant closure by completing an online form.

recalls.metaDescription: Creekwood Naturals recalls Pure Gum Spirits of Turpentine due to child poisoning risks. Secure your product and request a free replacement now.

recalls.Description: Diamond G Forest Products is recalling its 100% pure gum spirits of turpentine sold in 4-ounce clear glass bottles. These round bottles feature the label "Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine" and the company name. The product does not comply with the mandatory child-resistant packaging standards, posing a serious risk of poisoning to young children if ingested. Immediate action is necessary to prevent accidents.
recalls.Title: Diamond G Forest Products Issues Recall for Turpentine Bottles Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Diamond G Forest Products at 912-647-7463 between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at diamondgforestproducts@hotmail.com or visit www.diamondgforestproducts.com/recall-information for further details.

Products.Name: Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine

Images.Caption: Recalled Diamond G Forest Products Georgia Pine Gum Spirits of Turpentine Bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Diamond G Forest Products LLC, of Patterson, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Nature's Corner Market in Marietta, GA; Cornerstone Retreat in Gap, PA; Nickel Mine Health Foods in Paradise, PA; Leaves of The Trees in Memphis, TN; Country Boy's Mini Mall in Bristol, GA; and available online at Amazon

Hazards.Name: Non-compliant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children

Remedies.Name: Secure the turpentine bottles and contact Diamond G Forest Products for a free replacement child-resistant closure

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Diamond G Forest Products recalls turpentine bottles due to child poisoning risk. Secure bottles and contact for a replacement closure.

recalls.Description: Reckitt has announced a recall of Woolite Delicates detergent in 50-fluid-ounce bottles due to potential contamination with Pseudomonas species bacteria. This recall affects products with lot codes S24364, S24365, and S24366, which can be found printed on the back of the bottle. The bottles are identifiable by their white color and light pink label and cap, and were sold exclusively on Amazon.com.
recalls.Title: Reckitt Issues Recall for Woolite Delicates Detergent Due to Bacterial Contamination Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Reckitt at 800-228-4722 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at ConsumerCare_USA@reckitt.com, or visit https://www.woolite.us/Voluntary-Recall/ for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Woolite Delicates detergent, 50 fluid ounces
Recalled Woolite Delicates detergent, 50 fluid ounces and location of lot code and SKU

Manufacturers.Name: Reckitt Benckiser LLC, of Parsippany, New Jersey

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com in January 2025, priced between $14 and $17.

Hazards.Name: Risk of exposure to Pseudomonas species bacteria, which can cause serious infections in individuals with weakened immune systems.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the affected detergent and contact Reckitt for a full refund. To process the refund, write 'Recalled' on the back of the product, take a photo showing the UPC and lot code, and email it to ConsumerCare_USA@reckitt.com. Dispose of the product securely in household trash without emptying the bottle.

recalls.metaDescription: Reckitt recalls Woolite Delicates detergent due to bacterial contamination risk. Learn how to get a refund and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description: Taylor Water Technologies is recalling Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles that are part of the Taylor Phosphate Test Kits #K-1106 and those sold separately. These test kits are designed to measure phosphate levels in water for pools, spas, and aquariums. The affected bottles contain sulfuric acid and are labeled as "Phosphate Reagent #1" with part number "R-0980" and a volume of 22 mL (.75 oz). The shelf-life for these bottles is 24 months.
recalls.Title: Taylor Water Technologies Recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles Due to Chemical Burn Risk and Non-Compliant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Taylor Water Technologies at 800-837-8548 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or reach out via their online contact form at https://www.taylortechnologies.com/en/content/contact-customer-service. More information can be found by visiting https://www.taylortechnologies.com and selecting "Recall Information" under the Resource Center tab.

Products.Name: Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles from Taylor Phosphate Test Kits

Images.Caption: Recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle sold within the Taylor Phosphate Test Kits
Taylor Phosphate Test Kit with recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Taylor Water Technologies LLC, of Sparks, Maryland

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.taylortechnologies.com and other retailers, as well as in pool and aquarium stores nationwide from August 2022 through August 2024.

Hazards.Name: Risk of chemical burns due to non-child-resistant packaging of sulfuric acid

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles, secure them away from children, and contact Taylor for proper disposal and a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Taylor Water Technologies recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles due to chemical burn risks. Learn how to get a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Torquay eTrading LLC is recalling Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Essential Values Grip & Rip re-gripping kits due to potential poisoning hazards. These products, which include an 8-ounce bottle of solvent and 14 strips of golf tape, are misleadingly labeled as 'Safe & Non-Toxic' and lack necessary cautionary statements regarding petroleum distillates. Consumers are advised to stop using these items immediately.
recalls.URL: https://www.cpsc.gov/Recalls/2024/Torquay eTrading Recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and-Golf-Grip-Kits-Due-to-Risk-of-Poisoning-Violation-of-the-Federal-Hazardous-Substances-Act
recalls.Title: Recall of Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits Due to Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Torquay eTrading LLC at 347-587-8964 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email at recall@essentialvalues.us, or visit https://www.essentialvalues.us/pages/recall for more information.

Products.Name: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 26,000 (In addition, about 3,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Golf Grip Solvent, 8-Ounce
Recalled Grip and Rip Re-Gripping Solution Kit with 14 Strips of Grip Tape
Back of the Recalled Golf Grip Solution

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, eBay.com, Walmart.com, and www.essentialvalues.com from December 2017 to May 2024, priced around $9, with kits including golf grip tape available for approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Torquay eTrading LLC (dba Essential Values), of New York (formerly sold by Mocer LLC and Bright Ventures LLC)

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to misleading labeling and lack of cautionary statements

Remedies.Name: Stop using the products and contact Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label

recalls.metaDescription: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits recalled due to poisoning risk. Contact Torquay eTrading for corrective actions.

recalls.Description: Compare Brands is recalling the ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner, 30%, due to serious safety concerns. The product is packaged in a 16 oz. white bottle with a blue cap and features labeling that misrepresents its safety, claiming it is 'Safe to Use' without the necessary cautionary statements. This violation of the Federal Hazardous Substances Act poses a risk of poisoning and chemical burns to users. Consumers are urged to act promptly to ensure their safety.
recalls.Title: Recall of ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner Due to Poisoning and Chemical Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Compare Brands toll-free at 888-912-4622 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email support@comparebrands.net, or visit https://comparebrands.net/pages/product-recalls or https://comparebrands.net/ and select 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner, 30%

Images.Caption: Recalled ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner
Recalled ADIOS! Super Vinegar All Natural Cleaner (Label)

Manufacturers.Name: Compare Brands LLC, of Fort Wayne, Indiana

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from March 2023 through May 2024 for approximately $13.

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning and chemical burns due to misleading labeling.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately and contact Compare Brands for a free corrected label.

recalls.metaDescription: Compare Brands recalls ADIOS! Super Vinegar Cleaner due to poisoning risks. Contact them for a corrected label and safety information.

recalls.Description:
Kano Laboratories has issued a recall for several Super Lube products, including the Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant, Fire Resistant Hydraulic Fluid, and Metal Protectant & Corrosion Inhibitor. These items, designed to prevent corrosion in machinery, are sold in various sizes, including a four-ounce spray bottle, a one-quart trigger spray bottle, and a one-gallon container. The products are identifiable by their distinct packaging featuring a colorful stripe design. Specific lot numbers can be found on the bottom or rear labels of the containers.

The recalled products pose a significant risk due to non-child-resistant packaging, which violates the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. This could lead to serious poisoning incidents if children access the contents. Additionally, the presence of petroleum distillates raises concerns about potential lung damage if inhaled.
recalls.Title: Kano Laboratories Recalls Super Lube® Products for Poisoning Risk and Non-Compliance with Safety Packaging Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately keep the recalled Super Lube® products out of children's reach and contact Kano Laboratories for a full refund. Proof of possession is required, which can be submitted by emailing a photo of the product to info@super-lube.com. For further assistance, call 800-253-5823 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.super-lube.com/recall.

Images.Caption: Front Labels of Recalled Super Lube Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant
Rear Labels of Recalled Super Lube Syncopen Synthetic Penetrant

Manufacturers.Name: Kano Laboratories LLC, of Bohemia, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging; potential for lung damage from petroleum distillates.

Remedies.Name: Secure products out of children's reach and contact Kano Laboratories for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Kano Laboratories recalls Super Lube® products due to poisoning risks. Learn how to secure a refund and keep your family safe.

recalls.Description: Pure Brands of California has recalled its Purefog High Density Natural Fog Juice Liquid, designed for fog machines operating between 400-1500 Watts. This product, identified by model number B0BG4L3997 and Lot code #623-PFHD, is used to create fog effects commonly seen at concerts and events. The model and lot codes can be found on the back label of the container. The recall is due to concerns that the fog juice may expire earlier than the indicated expiration date, potentially posing health risks for individuals with weakened immune systems or mold allergies.
recalls.Title: Pure Brands of California Issues Recall for Purefog Juice Liquid Due to Mold Risk; Available Only on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Pure Brands of California at 888-622-6536 from 10 a.m. to 3 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at support@purebrandsoc.com, or visit http://purebrandsoc.com//recall.html for further details.

Products.Name: Purefog High Density Natural Fog Juice Liquid

Images.Caption: Recalled Purefog High Density Natural Fog Juice Liquid (model and lot number location)

Manufacturers.Name: Pure Brands of California, of San Diego, California

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from October 2023 to December 2023 for approximately $15.

Hazards.Name: Risk of respiratory or other infections due to potential early expiration and mold exposure.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately, dispose of any unused portions by pouring down the drain, and contact the company for a full refund. Amazon.com will also reach out to consumers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Purefog Juice Liquid from Pure Brands of California poses mold risk. Stop use and contact for refund. Available only on Amazon.

recalls.Description: Wet & Forget is recalling its "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover, available in 48 oz. and 68 oz. bottles with hose end nozzles. These blue plastic bottles may pose a risk as the nozzle clip can detach, potentially spraying the cleaning solution onto users, leading to skin and eye irritation. The affected products have UPCs 879288000077 for the 48 oz. size and 879288000084 for the 68 oz. size, located on the back label.
recalls.Title: Wet & Forget Recalls 2.7 Million Bottles of "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Remover Due to Skin and Eye Irritation Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Wet & Forget at 888-359-4623 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email nozzle@realtimeresults.net, or visit www.recallrtr.com/nozzle and select "Safety Recalls" for further details.

Products.Name: Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Outdoor Mold & Mildew Stain Remover

Images.Caption: Recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover – 48 oz. Bottle
Recalled Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover – 68 oz. bottle
Recalled hose-end nozzle sold with Wet & Forget "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Stain Remover

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 3,188 reports of dislodgement of the nozzle insert, including 157 reports of exposure to the cleaning solution and 28 reports that include complaints of skin or eye irritation.

Retailers.Name: Available at Ace Hardware, Costco, Home Depot, Lowes, True Value, Walmart, and various online platforms including www.amazon.com, www.SamsClubs.com, and www.walmart.com from February 2018 to November 2023, priced between $30 and $35.

Importers.Name: Wet & Forget (USA) Inc., of Elgin, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of skin and eye irritation due to dislodged nozzle clip

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product and contact Wet & Forget for a free replacement nozzle. Do not use the stain remover until the new nozzle is installed, and dispose of the original nozzle.

recalls.metaDescription: Wet & Forget recalls 2.7 million bottles of "Xtreme Reach" Mold & Mildew Remover due to potential skin and eye irritation risks.
